question: Compare the safety enhancement from integral RCS design (Ch.01) with the specific LOCA categories eliminated per Ch.05.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during all operational conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate the need for external power and large coolant loops, significantly lowering the risk of core damage and improving safety compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0174.title: DSRS 6.6, Rev 0: Inservice Inspection and Testing of Class 2 and 3 ComponentsII.3 Examination Categories and Methods
nuscale_ch01_0174.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale reactor design fully conforms to the regulatory requirements for inservice inspection and testing of Class 2 and 3 components, specifically regarding examination categories and methods. No exceptions or deviations are noted, indicating compliance with DSRS 6.6, Rev 0, Section II.3.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes special plant features of the NuScale Power Plant, highlighting structures such as the Central Utility Building and Diesel Generator Buildings, which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and equipment. It also emphasizes human factors engineering, noting that the plant design minimizes human error through automation, digital controls, and optimized workload management, in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0780.title: SRP 15.6.5.A, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of a Design Basis Loss-of-Coolant Accident Including Containment Leakage ContributionII.1 Calculated Doses and Containment Leakage Contribution
nuscale_ch01_0780.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of a design basis loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA), including containment leakage. It notes that while NuScale’s approach partially conforms to certain SRP 15.6.5.A guidance for dose calculations and containment leakage, the specific radiological acceptance criteria and analysis models are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3, and some guidance applies only to BWRs. Full conformance is noted for ESF system leakage assumptions.

nuscale_ch01_0779.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.4 Programmatic Requirements
nuscale_ch01_0779.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA) as outlined in DSRS 15.6.5 and related SRP sections. It notes full or partial conformance with requirements for calculating radiological consequences and containment leakage, with some criteria superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 and certain guidance applicable only to BWRs. The table documents how NuScale addresses each regulatory requirement for LOCA analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0170.title: SRP 6.5.4, Draft Rev 4: Ice Condenser as a Fission Product Cleanup SystemAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0170.summary: This section states that the NuScale reactor design does not use or credit an ice condenser containment or a pressure suppression pool, so the related SRP sections and criteria are not applicable. However, the NuScale design conforms to all requirements for inservice inspection and testing of Class 2 and 3 components as outlined in DSRS 6.6, including inspection intervals, accessibility, examination methods, and structural supports.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch01_0776.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.1 Evaluation of ECCS Performance
nuscale_ch01_0776.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCAs) and related radiological consequences. It notes that while some guidance and technical specifications are applicable, certain radiological acceptance criteria are superseded by more current regulatory sections. The NuScale design also supports exemptions from specific requirements, incorporating only technically relevant aspects into its safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both I&C and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch01_0775.title: SRP 15.6.4, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Main Steam Line Failure Outside Containment (BWR)All - Not Applicable
nuscale_ch01_0775.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequences of various accident scenarios. It notes that certain criteria, such as those for main steam line failure outside containment, are not applicable to NuScale’s design, while other requirements are either partially or fully met, with some acceptance criteria superseded by more current regulatory guidance. The document clarifies which standards apply to NuScale and where exemptions or alternative analyses are used.

nuscale_ch01_0682.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.4 AssumptionsWorst Case Failure of Single Active Component
nuscale_ch01_0682.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures inside and outside containment, specifically addressing the assumption of the worst-case failure of a single active component. The analysis confirms that NuScale meets all relevant criteria, including pressure, core damage potential, radiological limits, and safety system classification, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0681.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.3 AssumptionsPostulated Steam Line Break Effects
nuscale_ch01_0681.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures both inside and outside containment, specifically addressing the assumptions related to postulated steam line break effects. It confirms that NuScale meets all relevant criteria, including initial power level, plant operating mode, loss of offsite power, and worst-case single active component failure, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0662.title: DSRS 15.0.3, Rev 0: Design Basis Accident Radiological Consequence Analyses for the NuScale SMR DesignII.2 Control Room Radiological Habitability
nuscale_ch01_0662.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequence analyses during design basis accidents, specifically addressing control room radiological habitability. It confirms that the NuScale SMR design meets the relevant requirements of DSRS 15.0.3, ensuring adequate protection for control room personnel during postulated accidents. The section also references conformance with related standards and review plans.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with detailed specifications provided in referenced tables and sections.

nuscale_ch01_0811.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.5 Detect and Suppress Method Trip of reactor before SAFDL violation
nuscale_ch01_0811.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or detect-and-suppress (D&S) methods for thermal hydraulic stability; instead, existing reactor trip signals act preemptively to prevent violation of safety limits before flow instabilities can develop. The system partially conforms to some regulatory criteria by providing more limiting protection than required, but does not employ D&S systems or backup options, as reactor trip system (RTS) actions are considered sufficient.

nuscale_ch01_0810.title: DSRS 15.9.A, Rev 0: Thermal Hydraulic Stability Review ResponsibilitiesII.4 Detect and Suppress Method: Exclusion zone and buffer region methodology
nuscale_ch01_0810.summary: The NuScale design does not use the exclusion zone or buffer region methodology for thermal hydraulic stability, as reactor trip signals are designed to prevent conditions that could lead to unstable flow oscillations. Instead, the existing reactor trip system (RTS) provides protection by tripping the reactor before safety limits are violated, making additional detect and suppress (D&S) methods or backup options unnecessary. The RTS system partially conforms to some regulatory criteria by preventing instability conditions, but it does not actively detect or suppress instabilities.

nuscale_ch01_0663.title: DSRS 15.0.3, Rev 0: Design Basis Accident Radiological Consequence Analyses for the NuScale SMR DesignII.3 Technical Support Center Radiological Habitability
nuscale_ch01_0663.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequence analyses during design basis accidents, specifically addressing the Technical Support Center (TSC) radiological habitability. It notes that the TSC meets dose acceptance criteria when AC power is available, but its function is transferred to the main control room if AC power is lost. The section also references compliance with relevant sections of the Standard Review Plan and Design Specific Review Standard.

nuscale_ch01_0535.title: BTP 11-6, Rev 4: Postulated Radioactive Releases Due to Liquid-Containing Tank FailuresB.5 Exposure Scenarios and Acceptance Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0535.summary: This section states that the development of exposure scenarios and acceptance criteria for postulated radioactive releases due to liquid-containing tank failures is site-specific and the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant, not the NuScale design certification. Therefore, NuScale does not address these criteria, and compliance will be determined at the COL application stage.

nuscale_ch01_0777.title: DSRS 15.6.5, Rev 0: Loss-of-Coolant Accidents Resulting from Spectrum of Postulated Piping Breaks within the Reactor Coolant Pressure BoundaryII.2 Radiological Consequences of Most Severe LOCA
nuscale_ch01_0777.summary: This section discusses the NuScale design’s conformance with regulatory criteria for loss-of-coolant accidents (LOCA), specifically regarding the radiological consequences of the most severe LOCA. It states that NuScale’s approach conforms to the applicable Standard Review Plan (SRP) requirements, but for design certification applications, SRP Section 15.0.3 supersedes the radiological analysis criteria and methodologies in DSRS 15.6.5.

nuscale_ch01_0545.title: DSRS 12.2, Rev 0: Radiation SourcesII.4 NUREG-0737, Task Action Plan Item II.B.2
nuscale_ch01_0545.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to radiation sources and protection. It details which regulations and guidance documents are fully, partially, or not applicable, noting specific areas of conformance and referencing relevant tables for further information. The section also clarifies that some operational considerations are not applicable as they pertain to site-specific programs outside the scope of this report.
